channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 convicted in human smuggling case after Indian family froze to death on US-Canada border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — A jury convicted two men on Friday of charges related to human smuggling for their roles in an international operation that led to the deaths of a family of Indian migrants who froze while trying to cross the Canada-U.S. border during a 2022 blizzard.
item.dc:creator: MARK VANCLEAVE and MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas man killed by police while on 911 call
item.description: Brandon Durham, 43, of Las Vegas, died at the home on Tuesday, Nov. 12. Police said he was pronounced deceased at a hospital a short time thereafter.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, NewsNation

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs' third bid to be released on bail won't be decided until next week
item.description: A judge says he’ll rule next week on Sean “Diddy” Combs’ third request to be released from jail on bail while he awaits his sex trafficking trial.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cases of 'walking pneumonia' are rising across the US
item.description: Infections resulting in "walking pneumonia" are generally less severe than pneumonia, with many patients going through their daily routines.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, NewsNation

item.title: Officer kills pet dog mistaken for a coyote in Massachusetts town. The owner says it was unnecessary
item.description: An animal control officer shot and killed a pet dog in a Massachusetts town after mistaking it for a coyote in an incident local police are describing as a sad mix-up.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brazilian illegal immigrant wanted for drug trafficking arrested in Massachusetts sanctuary city
item.description: Federal immigration authorities appear to have stepped up arrests of illegal immigrant criminal suspects in Massachusetts in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about a Wisconsin man who faked his own death and fled to Eastern Europe
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer and left his wife and three children has been located in Eastern Europe and is communicating with law enforcement, but he has not committed to returning home, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas approves optional Bible-based lessons for public schools
item.description: Texas gave final approval Friday to optional biblical lesson plans for kindergarten through 5th grade classes in state public schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church, returned to mother
item.description: Three Utah children missing for two years have been located living with members of the Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, according to the Fredonia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, Fox 5

item.title: Prominent figure from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions found guilty
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — One of the most prominent figures from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions in 2022 has been found guilty on five counts including mischief and disobeying a court order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: McDonald's unveils new value menu, extends $5 Meal Deal
item.description: McDonald’s is offering its customers more ways to save on Big Macs, McNuggets, and other food items beginning in the New Year. The fast-food giant announced on Friday that it will launch a new everyday value menu on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump immigration crackdown: Denaturalization just a drop in the bucket
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming administration is expected to take aim at legal immigration, in addition to cracking down on the illegal variety, slowing the pace of application approvals and redirecting resources to look for fraud in old applications, including naturalizations.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KRON4

item.title: President of Atlanta's historically Black Spelman College steps down after leave of absence
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Spelman College's president won't be coming back from her leave of absence and is stepping down permanently, the historically Black women's college announced Thursday. The college, which has more than 3,000 students, hasn't said why Dr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church
item.description: It was suspected that the father had "orchestrated the disappearance and subsequent hiding" of the children.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, NewsNation

item.title: Cases of 'walking pneumonia' are rising across the US: Here's where it's worst
item.description: In October, the CDC wrote that since late spring, the number of infections caused by walking pneumonia has been increasing, especially among young children.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Storm inundates Northern California with rain, heavy snow. Thousands remain in the dark in Seattle
item.description: Heavy rain from a major storm prompted evacuation warnings for communities near a Northern California river that forecasters say could break its banks Friday, as the system continued to dump heavy snow in mountainous areas where some ski resorts opened for the season.
item.dc:creator: Godofredo A. Vásquez, Janie Har And Christopher Weber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump chooses loyalist Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Matt Gaetz withdraws
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Thursday he will nominate former Florida Attorney General Pam Bondi to lead the Justice Department, turning to a longtime ally after his first choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name from consideration amid scrutiny over sex trafficking allegations.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — More than 60 years after President John F. Kennedy was assassinated, conspiracy theories still swirl and any new glimpse into the fateful day of Nov. 22, 1963, in Dallas continues to fascinate.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alabama carries out nitrogen gas execution on inmate convicted in female hitchhiker's 1994 killing
item.description: Carey Dale Grayson, a 50-year-old Alabama inmate convicted in the 1994 murder of a female hitchhiker, was put to death on Thursday using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Number of women who are state lawmakers inches up to a record high
item.description: Women will for the first time make up a majority of state legislators in Colorado and New Mexico next year, but at least 13 states saw losses in female representation after the November election, according to a count released Thursday by the Rutgers Center for American Women and Politics.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A kayaker faked his own death and fled overseas. Now he's telling how he did it
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer so he could abandon his wife and three children has been communicating with authorities daily from Eastern Europe, even telling them how he did it, but has not committed to returning home, a sheriff said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disneyland Paris turns into real-life winter wonderland after season's 1st snowfall
item.description: The season’s first snowfall has turned Disneyland Paris into a real-life winter wonderland. Disney Parks shared a video on Thursday showing snow covering Main Street, U.S.A, Sleeping Beauty Castle, and all the festive holiday décor. It’s currently pouring snow at Disneyland Paris. pic.twitter.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US towns plunge into debates about fluoride in water
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For about 50 years, adding cavity-preventing fluoride to drinking water was a popular public health measure in Yorktown, a leafy town north of New York City. But in September, the town’s supervisor used his emergency powers to stop the practice.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYPD gets a new top cop after months of turmoil. Jessica Tisch is the 2nd woman to lead the force
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Moving to stabilize an administration roiled by investigations, resignations and his own indictment, New York City Mayor Eric Adams on Wednesday appointed sanitation chief Jessica Tisch as police commissioner.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amid X exodus, Bluesky CEO says 1M people per day joined in past week
item.description: The social media platform Bluesky has emerged as a popular alternative for users departing Elon Musk's X and other platforms, with nearly 1 million people joining each day over the past week, Bluesky CEO Jay Graber said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Almost 100 House Democrats push for Gaetz Ethics report’s release
item.description: Nearly 100 House Democrats wrote to the House Ethics Committee, urging them to publish its report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) and their investigation into his alleged misconduct after President-elect Trump nominated him to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to nominate professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is nominating the billionaire professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be secretary of the Education Department, tasked with overseeing an agency Trump has promised to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gabby Petito's family asks court to overturn police immunity in their civil case against Moab
item.description: The family of murder victim Gabby Petito is asking a Utah court to end the state's immunity for police officers as they attempt to move forward with a $50 million lawsuit against the Moab Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Scott Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Two women testified before the House Ethics Committee that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid them to have sex, according to the attorney representing them.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5
